chunk_summary: This section discusses hemoglobin thresholds and severity classification in India's Anemia Mukt Bharat program, highlighting WHO 2024 updates, clinical management strategies, and implications for national surveys and program dashboards.
learning_objectives: Understand the updated WHO 2024 hemoglobin thresholds and their implications for anemia classification.
Learn how hemoglobin thresholds influence clinical referral and programmatic decisions in India.
Recognize the importance of documenting the framework used for anemia classification in clinical and survey contexts.
Identify clinical signs of severe anemia requiring urgent stabilization and referral.
key_facts: WHO 2024 hemoglobin thresholds: <10.5 g/dL for 6–23 months, <11.0 g/dL for 24–59 months.
NFHS-5 Phase 2 reports anemia prevalence in children 6–59 months (<11 g/dL) at 67.1% nationally.
Hemoglobin thresholds influence school screening, referral urgency, and program dashboards in India.
Severe anemia should be treated as a clinical syndrome, with symptoms like tachycardia, respiratory distress, or shock prompting urgent care.
Comparing anemia prevalence across time periods requires consistent use of the same cutoff framework.
reference_quote: WHO updated hemoglobin cutoffs in 2024, introducing separate thresholds for 6–23 months (<10.5 g/dL) versus 24–59 months (<11.0 g/dL) and updating severity bands.
